title: caltha umbrosa
intro: Caltha umbrosa, also known as yellow marsh marigold, is a perennial plant species in the family Ranunculaceae. The plant contains a compound called ranunculin, which has been used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic qualities. It is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing fever, relieving digestive issues, and acting as a diuretic. Caltha umbrosa can be prepared as a herbal remedy in the form of a tea, infusion, or tincture, and it is also used in traditional medicine to treat a range of ailments.
